$doc: COMMITTED reference roster for the review council. It is NOT the runtime config: at setup, the installer reads the `presets` block and each role's `modelGuidance`/`transportPreference`, resolves each provider role to its CURRENT flagship model slug and the best available transport for THIS machine, then writes local/seats.<host>.json (gitignored). Model slugs are NEVER committed here — recipes use the {MODEL} placeholder so this file never goes stale. {EFFORT} = efforts.default below the critical tier, efforts.max at critical. {PROMPT_TEXT} = the review prompt content (arg transport); stdin transport omits it and pipes the prompt file. The engine (runner/doctor) reads minTier ONLY from the installed seats file; it never assumes the presets below — they are setup-time defaults.

presets.$doc: Setup-time defaults only. At setup the installer stamps each installed seat's `minTier` from here and writes the transportPreference order into the per-host candidate. DIVERSITY-FIRST rule: the OWN-provider seat is never the sole tier-1 reviewer — it gets minTier 2, and the strongest reachable FOREIGN lineage gets minTier 1, so the everyday (tier-1) review is cross-lineage. The flat map below is the Claude-host default (own=Opus at 2, lead-foreign=GPT at 1); on another host the own-provider and lead-foreign roles swap (Codex: GPT=2, Opus=1; Cursor/OpenCode: own=2, Opus=1). If no foreign seat is reachable at all, the own-provider seat falls back to minTier 1 and setup warns of reduced diversity. Foreign-strength order for the tier-1 slot: GPT > Grok > Gemini > GLM > DeepSeek > MiMo (Opus is strongest foreign on non-Claude hosts). No runtime code or instruction assumes these values; a hand-edited seats file may override them freely. A seat runs at council tier T iff seat.minTier <= T (absent minTier => 4 = critical-only).

tierComposition.$doc: Illustrative, for a Claude host with the default presets (own-provider Opus at minTier 2, lead-foreign GPT at minTier 1). On another host the own-provider and lead-foreign roles swap: the strongest FOREIGN lineage sits at low, the own-provider joins at elevated.

transportPreference.$doc: Per-role ordered list of transports, best first — this ordering is the RULE, not a suggestion: native (claude/codex) first, then cursor, then opencode (the universal catch-all that can carry every role via OpenRouter). The installer picks the first transport whose CLI is present AND whose driver smoke passes. An ABSENT CLI is skipped to the next transport (best-effort). A PRESENT CLI whose smoke FAILS (e.g. unauthenticated) HALTS setup — probable misconfiguration — it is NOT silently skipped. 'subagent' (opus on a Claude Code host only) is an in-process host subagent, not a CLI. 'claude'/'codex'/'cursor'/'opencode' are exec subprocesses via that CLI.

roster.modelGuidance: The Anthropic OPUS line specifically. Current target: opus-4.8 (the `opus` alias, which tracks the latest Opus, or a concrete claude-opus-* id such as claude-opus-4-8). NEVER Fable or Mythos (the Claude-5 / Mythos-class line) — setup must confirm the resolved slug is an Opus id.
roster.recursionDisable: --safe-mode (mechanically disables CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP) for the claude CLI / subagent; cursor --mode plan and opencode --agent plan are weaker read-only fallbacks in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root.

asExternalCursor.note: Opus-line model via Cursor when the claude CLI is absent (confirm the Cursor slug is an Opus id with `cursor-agent --list-models`). Isolation = read-only `--mode plan` in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root — weaker than `--safe-mode`; acceptable fallback.

asExternalOpencode.note: Opus-line model via OpenCode+OpenRouter when neither the claude CLI nor cursor-agent is available. {MODEL} is the OpenRouter anthropic slug (an Opus id); confirm the Opus-line rule still holds.

roster.modelGuidance: Leo's standing rule: the most capable flavor of the newest GPT generation. Current target: gpt-5.6-sol. GPT-5.6 ships three capability flavors — Sol > Terre > Luna (new names in 5.6, not lineages) — so 5.6 resolves to Sol, never Terre or Luna; a newer GPT generation supersedes 5.6 automatically and its most capable flavor is selected. Resolve the exact transport-specific slug at setup and never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: LEOS_COUNCIL_SEAT sentinel + --ephemeral + --sandbox read-only; normal CODEX_HOME is RETAINED for authentication (never override CODEX_HOME on a codex seat — it throws away host auth).

asNative.note: the host's own-provider GPT seat on a Codex host: a runner subprocess reusing the host's codex login (same CODEX_HOME, no separate key). Pins the setup-resolved OpenAI model per the flavor rule (5.6 → Sol).

asExternal.note: On any non-Codex host, resolve per the flavor rule in modelGuidance (5.6 → Sol). Uses the host's normal authentication. --ephemeral prevents session files; the inherited LEOS_COUNCIL_SEAT sentinel mechanically refuses nested Leo councils.

asExternalCursor.note: GPT/OpenAI model via Cursor when the codex CLI is absent: resolve per the flavor rule in modelGuidance (5.6 → Sol), then confirm the exact Cursor slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`. Isolation = read-only `--mode plan` in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root instead of an isolated CODEX_HOME.

asExternalOpencode.note: GPT via OpenCode+OpenRouter when neither codex nor cursor-agent is available. {MODEL} is the OpenRouter openai slug; resolve per the flavor rule.

roster.modelGuidance: Latest Grok flagship. Current target: grok-4.5. For cursor-agent, confirm the exact slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`; for opencode use the OpenRouter xai slug. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only) or opencode --agent plan (read-only) — both in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root, so repo-local agent config never loads, including during self-review.

roster.modelGuidance: Latest GLM flagship. Current target: glm-5.2. Via Cursor (confirm the Cursor GLM slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`) or OpenRouter for the opencode transport. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only) or opencode --agent plan (read-only) — both in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root.

asExternalCursor.note: GLM via Cursor (preferred when available) — only if `cursor-agent --list-models` lists a GLM slug; {MODEL} is the Cursor slug, not the OpenRouter one.

asExternalOpencode.note: GLM via OpenCode+OpenRouter when cursor-agent is absent. {MODEL} is the OpenRouter zhipu slug.

roster.modelGuidance: Latest Gemini flagship. Current target: gemini-3.1-pro. Via Cursor (confirm the Cursor Gemini slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`) or OpenRouter for the opencode transport. Native Gemini-CLI plan mode is experimental, so Cursor or OpenCode+OpenRouter is the firm route. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only) or opencode --agent plan (read-only) — both in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root

asExternalCursor.note: Gemini via Cursor (preferred when available) — only if `cursor-agent --list-models` lists a Gemini slug; {MODEL} is the Cursor slug, not the OpenRouter one.

asExternalOpencode.note: Gemini via OpenCode+OpenRouter when cursor-agent is absent. {MODEL} is the OpenRouter google slug.

roster.modelGuidance: Xiaomi MiMo flagship. Current target: mimo-v2.5-pro. OpenCode+OpenRouter ONLY — cursor-agent has no verified MiMo slug. The OpenRouter slug is xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: opencode --agent plan (read-only) in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root. Neither loads CLAUDE.md-style council mandates.

asExternalOpencode.note: MiMo via OpenCode+OpenRouter only. {MODEL} is the OpenRouter xiaomi slug (mimo-v2.5-pro). Dropped entirely if opencode is not installed or its smoke fails.

roster.modelGuidance: DeepSeek flagship. Current target: deepseek-v4-pro. OpenCode+OpenRouter ONLY — cursor-agent has a known deepseek-v4-pro incompatibility (reasoning_content replay breaks long tool chains; 200K context cap) while opencode passes the full 1M. The OpenRouter slug is deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: opencode --agent plan (read-only) in the runner-provided scratch cwd with a synthetic Git root. Neither loads CLAUDE.md-style council mandates.

asExternalOpencode.note: DeepSeek via OpenCode+OpenRouter only (cursor is excluded due to its reasoning_content bug). {MODEL} is the OpenRouter deepseek slug (deepseek-v4-pro). Dropped entirely if opencode is not installed or its smoke fails.

$runtimeSchema.$doc: Shape of the machine-local local/seats.<host>.json the installer writes and the council skill/runner reads. There is NO top-level `native` object: every reviewer — including the host's own-provider seat — is one element of `seats[]`. A seat with mode `subagent` is an in-process host subagent (Claude Code only) dispatched by the orchestrator; a seat with mode `exec` is a runner subprocess via argv (every other harness, including an own-provider seat on Codex/Cursor/OpenCode). `seats` is ordered strongest-first; `minTier` (1..4, default 4) is the minimum council tier at which a seat runs — a seat runs at tier T iff minTier <= T. A config with zero seats is valid (council skips with a reduced-diversity note).

seats.name: path-safe unique seat id, e.g. opus | gpt | glm | gemini | grok | mimo | deepseek
seats.provider: anthropic | openai | zhipu | google | xai | xiaomi | deepseek | custom; copied from the catalog roster identity, not inferred from the display name
seats.minTier: optional integer 1..4 (default 4 = critical-only); a seat runs at council tier T iff minTier <= T
seats.model: (subagent only) resolved model, e.g. opus or claude-opus-4-8
seats.argv: (exec only) resolved command array, {MODEL} already resolved; {EFFORT} and {PROMPT_TEXT} remain as runtime placeholders
seats.adapter: optional exec-seat adapter, allow-listed: claude | codex | opencode | cursor-json | raw (raw is explicit-only — an unknown binary without an adapter is an invalid seat); cursor requires cursor-json after an output-contract smoke test
seats.responsePath: cursor-json exec seat only: dotted JSON string field containing reviewer text
seats.timeoutSeconds: optional per-seat wall-clock limit in seconds, integer 1..900 (default 300)
seats.planTimeoutSeconds: optional exec-seat plan-checkpoint wall-clock limit in seconds, integer 1..900; catalog flagship transports use 600; ignored for implementation seats and the reduced-diversity fallback after unconditional validation
seats.cwd: optional per-seat working-directory mode: "scratch" (default — the runner creates a private dir with its own synthetic Git root under local/council/work/<run>/ and removes it after the seat, so parent-repo config like .cursor/rules or AGENTS.md never loads into a reviewer even during self-review; the reviewed repo's absolute path is injected as a prompt header) | "repo" (run inside the reviewed repo — opt-out for transports that cannot read outside their workspace; re-opens repo-local instruction injection)
seats.env: optional inline string:string env overlaid on the inherited process env; NON-SECRET only — secret-named keys (TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD/API_KEY/PRIVATE_KEY) are refused at install. For secrets, use envFile.
seats.envFile: optional path to a .env file relative to LEOS_LOCAL (e.g. council/env/gpt.env), gitignored and mode 0600; secret-named keys ARE allowed in it. The runner loads and merges it into the seat subprocess env at dispatch; its contents never appear in prompts, logs, or result.json. If omitted, the runner still looks for local/council/env/<name>.env conventionally.

$runtimeSchema.smokeTests: {name: {passed: bool, confirmedAt: int}} stamped by leos-seats.py write for every mode:exec seat whose driver smoke was confirmed
